In Baghdad today, the Iraqi parliament voted to oust its outspoken speaker.
Mahmoud Mashhadani is a leader of the main Sunni Arab bloc in the legislature.
The parliament said his behavior had been an embarrassment.
He frequently engaged in shouting matches with other members of the assembly.
NPR's Jamie Tarabay joins us now from Baghdad.
Jamie, the speaker has been under pressure for sometime now.
What happened that finally pushed the parliament to vote him out of office?
It was basically a scuffle that happened yesterday at the end of the session where a Shiite Turkoman member complained that he'd been roughed up by some of Mashhadani's bodyguards.
And that, at one point, Mashhadani didn't even tried to stop his guards, the men handling this parliament member.
Jamie, Mashhadani is, if I understand, a former physician.
Can you tell us a little bit more about him?
